name: OKPPT MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for PowerPoint/PPT presentation creation and manipulation — enabling AI agents to create slide decks, add and modify slides, insert text and images, apply themes and layouts, and generate complete presentations from scratch. Integrates presentation creation capabilities into agent workflows for automated reporting, pitch decks, and content production.
use_cases: Generating complete PowerPoint presentations from outlines from content agents
Creating business reports and data visualizations as slides from reporting agents
Building pitch decks and proposal presentations from business automation agents
Adding slides and modifying existing presentations from document agents
Automating recurring presentation updates from operations agents
not_for: Complex data visualization requiring precise chart formatting (use dedicated chart tools)
Google Slides users (this targets PowerPoint format)
Teams needing collaborative real-time presentation editing
best_when: An agent needs to generate or modify PowerPoint presentations programmatically — creating reports, briefings, or pitch decks as part of automated workflows.
avoid_when: You use Google Slides or Keynote as your primary presentation tool — this targets the PowerPoint/PPTX format.

auth.notes: No authentication required for local PowerPoint generation. File-based operations — reads and writes PPTX files locally.

pricing.notes: Free open source PowerPoint generation tool. No API costs — uses python-pptx or similar libraries locally.

known_agent_gotchas: PowerPoint formatting is complex — pixel-perfect layouts may require iteration
Image insertion requires local image paths — URL-based images may need downloading first
PPTX schema is complex; not all PowerPoint features may be supported
Slide numbering and master slide management can be tricky to automate
Community MCP with limited documentation — test with simple presentations first
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local file operations only. No network calls. No credentials. PPTX files are stored locally. Ensure file write paths are in intended directories — no path traversal.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local only. No credentials. File write operations — validate paths. No network exposure.
